reviews.review_description:
I really don't dmunderstand how this games doesn't have more downloads. Big VR youtubers are always covering the same games and yet something like this that actually looks great, is super polished and has lots of replayability somehow goes under the radar.

I would highly recommend it even at full price (which is what I bought it for). Its challenging but super therapeutic and addicting to play. The feeling of smoothly completing a mission is something you don't get anywhere else.

-Astro

This is one of those games that is way more than it seems to be when you read the description … description sounds a little boring, like it’s a campaign game where you go pick stuff up and you move it back to your depot.  Great… how fascinating, right?

But the physics is so good and the little space craft is so immersive … that it becomes a really mesmerising experience.  I have played the whole game all the way through and I still go jump back into it and just play random missions … for the sort of meditative obsessive engagement of itt!  it’s as close as I’m ever gonna get to actually flying a tiny excursion module in space and it’s so much fun.  I customised the controls so they were intuitive for me, and it’s really fun when it becomes 2nd nature and you’re pulling off fancy 6dof manoeuvres as naturally as riding a bike.

Space suit games make the headset feel natural, too,

 I’ve had some chitchat with the developers and they were really friendly and really helpful … and I just can’t recommend it enough . this is a real sleeper… just get it and try it … it is incredibly immersive and engaging, and also there’s a certain amount of humour involved as well.  it’s got a very laid-back vibe and yet it’s quite challenging￼￼.  Solid gold.  All I want is more missions — space rescue or tow truck would be favourite.

reviews.review_title: This is my exact type of autism
reviews.review_description:
Class act game. 

The graphics are fantastic, they're sharp and realistic and it is a beautiful sight to behold the cosmos

it's completely physics and momentum based with full reliance on motion controls, and there is a great suite of customisation and accessibility options to tailor it to your needs, making it much more feasible for those who aren't as willing to fine tune it so much right out the gate. 

As I said, this is my exact kind of autistic fixation, god bless you Devs. 

Don't sleep on this, seriously.

This game doesn't look much on the screenshots. It's about moving cargo from one place to another. Doesn't sound very interesting, either.
But it's the most immersive and engrossing experience I've had in vr for ages. And it's funny. 
And look at the price! That's ridiculous! 
Don't nip out for cup of coffee. Use the money on this instead, and thank me later.
EDIT:
After playing this for a couple of days, one thing I will say is, it seems to drain the battery pretty quickly. There's a lot of complex physics going on in the background, so probably expected. Not a complaint, just an observation.
I'm finding I want to replay missions to get higher up the leaderboard, so this should have good replay-ability, even when you've finished. 
Some people are completing missions in a few minutes, which is staggering! I'm pretty hopeless right now, but it's not stopping me loving it!
Only about an hour in, so much fun.  Funnily I wasn't dizzy in the game, but trying to walk afterwards felt pretty comical.
Such a cool game! I've only played one session but I had to write a review.

Space Docker is an absolute gem of a game! The in-game physics are phenomenal, providing a truly immersive experience that feels like you're actually in space. The controls are intuitive once you get the hang of them, allowing for full 360-degree movement and smooth strafeing using both yokes. When you step into the VR cockpit, the sensation is just magical—like you're truly floating among the stars.

It might take a bit of perseverance to master the controls, especially if you're new to space physics, but it's absolutely worth it. Overall, it's a fantastic game that offers a beautiful escape into the cosmos. I do wish it were more expansive, but for the price, it’s an incredible experience.

Chill game, well made, smooth framerate always. You may want to enable the vignette for crashing at least.

Recommend right thumb stick for strafe right hand for orientation, and left stick only for front-back thrust. 

Still, I think sleep between sessions is required to improve xD.

reviews.review_title: Someone saw the dark side of the moon
reviews.review_description:
Day 1: Totally unable to control the vehicle.
Day 2: Still totally unable to take a proper turn.
Day 3: Finally got the grasp and started playing.

I enjoyed a lot the flying mechanics and found the docking pretty interesting. But thats until i reached the first "time trial" level.. After that, MAN I was hooked.
I found my self floating in space, working for what it was supposed to be done, listening to some FANTASTIC guitar solo tunes that remind me of Pink Floyd and thinking "Oh man! Thats my dream job!!!" I'm a sucker for some GOOD music in video games and I'm a musician myself. I havent completed any more levels cause I felt the urge to write this review. I cant really write enough cause only someone who loves pink floyd and space can trully understand how AWESOME it felt. I had my most WOW moment in my vr gaming experience playing this  gem that you should totally buy blindfolded!!!
DEVS! If you read this, please!!! We need more of this Pink Floydish psychedelic rock/blues tunes!!
Excellent work!!!
Bravo!!!

reviews.review_title: The best VR cockpit sim on the quest!
reviews.review_description:
My new favorite on the quest, do not miss out on this if you like space/flight/vehicle simulations with vr cockpits!

100% newtonian if you disable all assists. Very challenging but fun!

What an awesome little game! You are piloting a small shuttle just to transport cargo containers. Basically a cross between a tug boat and a forklift, but in zero gravity with space shuttle controls. Your left stick thrusts you forward and backwards and strafes up, down, left and right, while the right stick controls your pitch and rotation. You line up the ship with docking points on the containers and smoothly dock with them for transport. It's a little disorienting at first but I was surprised how fast it "clicks" and it becomes more a matter of mastering the controls and controlling your momentum in zero-g to be as fast and smooth at your job as possible.

And the game is great at making this feel like your job (just a very fun one), with the ship's cockpit designed to feel like an old school (but in the future/space- think of the mining ships from the Alien movies) piece of equipment. 

An excellent "blue collar, wipe the sweat and git 'er done country rock meets tripping through the cosmos" soundtrack and funny, job orientation live action training videos really help to sell the feel.

Addicting, fun and extremely satisfying to play and improve your skills with leaderboards and weekly challenges, and I felt compelled to write this recommendation on the chance that it helps anyone get introduced to it.
No multiplayer you only play solo not with frends in same host

developer_response.body:
Yes - there is no multiplayer.
Why did you think there would be?

reviews.review_description:
Firstly, this sim flies like you are actually in space, not like an aeroplane in space. 
Secondly, the beginner controls are setup to help you cancel out relative velocity easily, but you can turn that off to go hard core. 
Thirdly, you are a forklift driver, IN SPACE!!  
Good intro training modules get you into the swing of things, so it’s not too intimidating, and then you are off slinging cargo pods and trying to get your quota done before your shift ends and you are on unpaid overtime!  
And as a tech demo it feels very polished, and I look forward to more features bing added. 
Great SIM!

reviews.review_title: Perhaps the best game on VR
reviews.review_description:
When I first played this a giant grin plastered over my face. The gameplay and cockpit and the design of this game are very rewarding. 
It’s very relaxing to play and quickly becomes addictive. 

The graphics, levels, gameplay, design and music are all great. 

It’s easily one of the most rewarding experiences on VR. 

If you like space or relaxing puzzlers you’ll enjoy it. 

I hope the devs are working on a sequel, and considering expand this world. 

There space navigation itself is so satisfying and rewarding (ability to strafe) it becomes a real thrill piloting your ship. There’s so much potential. I’d like to have missions - maybe an open world. Pick up or transport people. Navigate between missions by flying there. A story etc. even multiplayer (work together to fix a ship or something). All with the same core gameplay mechanic - Newtonian physics in a strafing space docker

And please more levels!

Remind me of when elite dangerous just came out and everybody was review bombing it because the tutorial without any of the computer assist was hard.

With all the computer assist it is STILL challenging.  This is a game that takes time to learn.

It’s supposed to be hard.  It’s going to be hard.  It takes a lot of effort to learn.  If you want to start without any computer assist, expect to be stuck in the tutorial for a long time.  Getting vr joysticks will help a lot.  Having some real world resistance will help you with feedback.

There isn’t a whole lot to this game, but it’s got what it has down to a T.  Excellent experience.  5/5 stars.  Highly recommend.

For the devs:  PLEASE KEEP IT UP.  I really hope you turn this into an expanded experience.  Take exactly what you have here, and then add planets and stuff.  Basically make Elite Dangerous but for VR.  You already have so much down perfect.

I am eager to see what’s next.

I’m pretty smitten by these kinds of games - single player, simple graphics, good music kind of games. The way you can just be fully immersed and chill out in another world. I get the same vibe from DigVR which also has 2 virtual joysticks to control movement. 

If you are nerdy like me and want to hone your zero gravity flying skills then Space Docker VR is for you!

reviews.review_title: A shame there’s nothing else like it
reviews.review_description: It’s currently the only Quest standalone space flight game with Newton-style physics. Your mission? Sit back, relax, turn on your favorite music, and work safely. Try to collect all the S.T.A.R.Z., then beat the developer’s time — that’s when you’ll truly feel how fun and satisfying this game is. For speed lovers, there’s a racing mode where you can master drifting in space. You’ll also find an endless mode with random work scenarios — perfect for practice or just chilling out. To keep things fresh, new weekly jobs bring exciting challenges. And as you explore, you’ll discover surprise tools that will definitely catch you off guard!

What a great game!  Controls are spot on, skill curve is rewarding, and there's plenty to come back to and try and improve scores. But also you can just sit back (recommend playing in an office chair) and relax and move some crates around, or search for unlockables. 
Two things I'd love to see:
Coop mode. Give each player distinct abilities, or can each only move a certain type of crate but need to work together to free them. Lots of opportunities here. 

And a push throttle (like a plane) for controlling speed. I find I often grab the stick in the wrong position and can't physically tilt the speed stick back enough to slow down or reverse. 

Other than that this is a superbly put together game, great simplistic graphics on the quest that work well, and all the controls feel great to interact with. Well done devs!  Please continue adding to this game.

One of the biggest standout for me with this title was honestly just how enjoyable it is to transport cargo (especially once you get past the learning curve of the controls). The controls will feel clunky in the beginning but the more you play and get beyond the learning curve the cleaner they begin to feel. This can lead to some truly satisfying moments. Did a brief review on my YouTube channel. PR-IN-VR on YouTube. If your prone to motion sickness this is probably not gonna be for you but if your the type who is willing to tough it out. You’ll def build you “VR legs” with this one.

reviews.review_title: Very fun and very difficult and worth it
reviews.review_description:
This game is challenging and extremely fun, it’s to bad there isn’t an option for multi player or the ability to upgrade the ship with better boosters and a different docking configuration that’s more visually direct 
This game deserves 5 stars  after the boss told me I might as well stay till my next shift starts because everyone else finished and went home hours ago but I was still doing a great job!   It made me laugh and sorry for myself at the same time

I did NOT find this relaxing at all, as some people have stated. I could t even complete the tutorial. Lined up the container, all on green, approach and everything goes red and out of alignment. After 50 minutes on the second tutorial container, (the one that spins until you stop it with the limpets) I gave up. I don’t need this stress in my life!

developer_response.body: Fair enough :) it definitely can be stressful

reviews.review_description:
I hate most standalone VR games, because they’re usually low quality cash grabs, with rubbish graphics and lazy devs. 

This is the complete opposite, it’s the first game I’ve seen on quest that has the option to turn up the graphics and the FPS in game. Quest games optimiser is not needed, everything looks perfect. 

The controls and movement, again. Done perfectly. If you don’t like the custom controls there’s even an option to bind actions to different buttons. 

I’ve paid £50 for games and got zero enjoyment out of them, so I was very surprised when I picked this up on sale for £3, with how good it is. 

They should really stop pushing things like gorilla tag to the front page and start putting quality well made games like this at the top of the library so people don’t skim past it. 

I don’t currently have PCVR, and I got into quest to play elite dangerous, so this will certainly help scratch that itch until I can get a decent gaming PC.

I used to play Elite Dangerous VR and this game is similar to the docking aspect of that game.  I completed all of the Space Docker VR missions in a few days except for the last one which was too difficult for me.  I would gladly pay for additional missions!  Please!

Super immersive.
There's something so zen about navigating zero G space, lining up, docking, hauling and stowing stuff. Handling nukes, blowing stuff up, ramming stuff.
Controller integration and customization is superb.
The only annoyance really is that I have to restart the headset to quit or access any native device functionality.
Otherwise a phenomenal game experience that melts the hours away.

Really enjoying this so far. The gameplay is fun and challenging, but not frustratingly so. The tutorials are great. As a taller guy, I love the options in the menu to move your body and the controls! There are a few games that are difficult to play because interactive elements are behind my arms, but with a 5 second adjustment, I'm comfortable in this.

My only complaint is that I can't find a button to close the game itself, and opening the Quest desktop drops me to 1 frame in ~10 seconds (Quest 2). I have to sit with my arm pointed at the "quit" button, hope it's lined up enough to register, and then continuously pull the trigger to get it to close. I even had to just reboot one time because it stopped responding.

It's strangely relaxing. A simple game but very well done and very immersive. I really like the dark level. Would be great if there would be a construction element added to the game, like building a modular space station.

Working my way through the training missions, and starting to get the hang of the game after a few hours of play.
I picked this game up when it was on offer, taking a chance (I have had to stop playing a number of VR games due to feeling motion sickness, but interestingly not with this game, in fact I am loving the realistic 6 degrees of freedom ship piloting experience (with piloting practise the motion must make sense / be less confusing than I have experienced with some other games?) Definitely is well worth the normal non sale price … great job developers 👍

reviews.review_title: On the fence, not impressed so far.
reviews.review_description:
I bought this game because of the great reviews and it seemed like something very unique, unlike anything else in my library. Gotta say, I'm a bit underwhelmed so far.

In the tutorial, going through the basic commands. I'm prompted to pitch the nose up and down, yet the repeated motion shown with the right controller is the counter-clockwise and clockwise twist, which I believe is the yaw and not the pitch. In any case, the nose NEVER pitched up, but after about a minute, it pushed me forward to the next step anyway: to pitch the nose down. I was shown the opposite yaw, which of course never caused the nose to pitch down. Again, I was pushed forward through the tutorial without ever having learned the command for pitch.

Also, I'm not sure what the terminology for this is and whether or not ai can even articulate it correctly, but when I push forward or backward enough with the thrust, it's almost as if I need to l let go of the handle and "reset" it back to its original position to go in the reverse direction...almost as if there is not enough range and it doesn't spring back to a neutral position on its own.

I selected the "normal" difficulty for the movement controls because I like a challenge but now I'm thinking I should go back and start on easy. I'll give it a shot again in the next few days but this may end up being a refund for me.

developer_response.body:
Hey there - this doesn't sound normal.
 -- 
(my apologies in advance for the way this is formatted, this text-box doesn't support multiple-paragraphs)
 -- 
The default pitch bind should indeed be a back-and-forth motion on the right stick like every other flight/space game.
 -- 
If it's asking you to twist (i.e. as if you were unscrewing a bottle cap) indicates something has gone quite wrong. 
Almost like the ship's controls are bound incorrectly.
 -- 
My suggestion is to press the "reset to default" at the top middle of the controls menu (you should be safe to reset all the sub-panels too, since you've only just started playing).
You can also verify which stick movements correspond to which axes in there too.
 -- 
I have no idea how that could happen in the first place, but it's the first time we've ever heard of that issue out of 12k+ players.
Thanks for letting me know in either case, since it'll be important for me to investigate if there's an underlying system issue.

-------

The tutorial mission's holograms should respect your personal binds, and once the settings have been reset, it should show a back-and-forth motion. 
 -- 
If it doesn't or if there are any other issues, please let me know at tombailey@catcommandos.com 
(or if you prefer, our discord has a support ticket system where you can create a 1-on-1 ticket for us to look into this for you - our discord can be found at: https://discord.gg/9vdMTkPWhA)
 -- 
There's no AI in the tutorial to speak ok, it'll only move on when it detects that you've done a certain amount of pitching, but I can imagine that eventually, with enough wriggling, it could trigger, so that's not your fault.

-------
As for the centering issue you seem to describe, this is a fairly common issue with all "virtual axis" VR joysticks (i.e. VTOL VR and co.), where the lack of center reference can lead to a perceived drift.
The way to fix this is to occasionally let go of the joystick, let your hands relax into a neutral position, and re-grab it. It's a bit of a quirk of these type of games, but you get used to it quick.
 -- 
My apologies that your first go with this game has been marred with this tech issue, I just hope this info helps, and that you can enjoy it more once it's all resolved :)
 -- 
- TB

reviews.review_title: Well done with great attention to detail
reviews.review_description:
VR is a perfect place for creating unique and original ideas and this game does not disappoint. If moving cargo in space existed, it'd probably be a lot like this. It takes a while to grasp the physics and controls, but this game is loads (pun intended) of fun! Graphics, sound, and gameplay is perfect. 

I accidentally bumped a crate out into deep space and almost gave up, but I followed it and took my time grabbing it to bring it all the way back and it was so rewarding! I'm only on the third level but look forward to playing all of them! 
Do not overlook this game!
I have played for about a hour and it's just amazing the graphics the movement the everything it's just amazing

Reminds me a lot of Lunar Flight from the DK2 days, so if you remember that fondly then this is a must-buy. I originally bought this on Steam and played dozens of hours, hadn’t even noticed that it was native on Quest until today. Its plays every bit as well, if not better. The controls feel really well tuned to the Q3, and it’s great to be able to play it without firing up the PC.

Honestly, this is probably my favorite VR game—quick to pick up for a short session, easy to get lost in, very flow state inducing. Bravo!

Yes , it's physics based but the thrust needs to be on a separate button . Take a look at the game End Space , THATS HOW A SHIP SHOULD HAVE ITS CONTROLS!! Not this garbage , where thrust and maneuver are competing . Its almost impossible to control properly.  I am cool with extremely difficult but this is pure nonsense. Not worth buying unless you want to convince yourself this is some kind of challenge worthy of your time. It really isn't, though.  I loved the idea , it reminded me of Space Miner for Gear VR or Oculus GO several years ago but I am immensely disappointed with this game.

developer_response.body: Did you try rebinding the controls? :)

reviews.review_description: FANTASTIC! I have one question, when I beated one dev's time on racing level (Faster than light achievement) all of my 3stars 'XXX' changed to 'DEV' inscription in levels select section. Its coreect operation?  Thanks for answer and for perfect game!

developer_response.body:
Hey there! glad you're enjoying the game :)

You'll see "DEV" instead of XXX if, #1 you've beaten a dev time and 2# you've enabled the "time attack tools" unlock :)

a DEV rating for that level means that you beat our time for that level.
Speaking of which well done!! :D not many players can say they've done that!

reviews.review_description: I loved it!

It is surprisingly fun and beautiful, despite looking kinda "sparse" and simple. It feels really immersive and realistic. The controls are very intuitive. When I'm too lazy to play intense games, I play this. It's kinda therapeutic.


Edit: I forgot to mention about the OS windows bug -- the system slows to a crawl when pressing the Oculus button (on Quest 2), taking several minutes for the OS windows to show up, and pressing the Quit button is even more tedious, since the controller/pointer updates at ~0.1 Hz. I wish there's an easier way to quit the game. It's likely due to lack of memory, likely thrashing the hell out of the SSD. Survival Nation used to have this problem too.
Would love if they added an exit game button in the cockpit. Also, would enjoy being able to bind roll to the L and R triggers and pitch to A/B

developer_response.body:
Hi - We're really sorry about the home button issue. It was caused by a Meta OS update last in September and seems to be on a select set of customer devices, which makes it hard to figure out. 

The Meta support engineers are telling is it looks like an issue with their universal menu, rather than something directly being caused by us, but unfortunately, our game seems to trigger it somehow.

If you want some extra help, you can reach out to us on our discord and we can see what we can do for you :)

EDIT: We do have a discord link on our site, but my apologies for not leaving it here first time. It's here :) https://discord.com/invite/9vdMTkPWhA

reviews.review_title: Great game, shame about one thing
reviews.review_description: I love this game, it's a nice relaxing game the only issue is closing the game down, I have to shut the quest down to get out of the game which is annoying.

developer_response.body:
Hey there! :) Glad you enjoyed the game.  We just did a fix for the closing down issue.
Annoyingly it was caused by a meta OS update, but should be all good now!

reviews.review_title: Very Well Done - Excellent Physics Fun
reviews.review_description: This game is very well produced - much better than I expected.  The controls are well thought out and easy to operate.  Dealing with frictionless physics can be daunting, but this game makes it fun.  It almost becomes a form of meditation - where you have to keenly focus your attention on a gentile touch and thoughtful precision.  Nice job!
brilliant little gem, i love it.
Picked this up on sale for $7 and I’m blown away with good it feels! Once I got my head around the controls it felt so real and responsive. Now I’m on the grind to get on top of those leaderboards

Great game. So relaxing to just do a simple task that is so difficult. 

Game works great. Only things is that if you put the graphic all the way up you cant leave the game with Quest button. 

Other than that its perfect.  Love the graphic.

**** update 17 days later: 
Still coming back to this game. Putting it on the hardest difficulty levels makes it insane. Especially with the moving crates and black holes. Took me litteraly an hour to dock. 

It really feels that I achieved something. 

Must buy for people who love realism.

This game is like the better version of "Space Salvage". The controls are on point, which is necessary for the pinpoint precision needed to dock your ship perfectly. If you were looking for a space ship sim game, look no further. You won't regret getting this game.

I only ask the developer for more ships to drive (larger, with greater complexities), more objectives, just more of everything xD

I just happened to come across this game and give it a go and I'm sure glad I did. I don't know what flying an aircraft in space would be like but if imagine it's like this. The controls feel so good to fly around and the game looks good. Definitely makes you feel like a space trucker. If I only have one criticism it's that there should a quit game option in the game. My quest menu won't come up to quit the game so I'm left having to restart the quest every time I want to get off the game. A bit of an inconvenience but still an overall fantastic game

The physics and controls in this game are really well designed and implemented and their difficulty level settings make it fun at any experience level. I just can’t get into orbits around the black holes no matter what I try. A better guide there would be good. Also, how do you quit out of the game? There’s no option for it in the menus and when you press the Meta Quest button the MQ overlay doesn’t show. I have to hard restart my device. ￼

developer_response.body:
Hey there! :) Glad you enjoyed the game.  We just did a fix for the overlay issue.
Annoyingly it was caused by a meta OS update, but should be all good now!

reviews.review_title: Deserves to be in the main store!
reviews.review_description:
Wow, I had this wislisted for a while but bought it only recently. It’s an awesome space trucking game where the newtonian physics will really get you spinning if making any wrong moves. Perfect blend of being challenging - that will require your full attention - to a relaxing space meditation experience. Easily worth 5+ stars!!!

I just wish there would be a way to play my own mp3 tracks, and an option to turn off the speed vector indicator (to be even more challanging) to force you to only rely on debris to get an understanding of current direction.

Highly recommended!

reviews.review_title: Please sir, Could I have some more?
reviews.review_description: Exactly what I've been searching for for a put on your own tunes and pilot in 0 gravity game. Another review stated to put on the interstellar theme and go to town, and I will second that instruction and also encourage using your favorite playlists to vibe out in this great and simple game that's actually fun and feels great to play. I'd also love more levels, and would buy another game with this ship to just pilot around in caves, underwater maybe and through distant planets with strange new biomes and such if its  not to much to ask for...

It's one of my all time favourite games, but I only wish it had more levels, or at least an open world multiplayer mode to play with other players. You can get bored pretty fast after going through everything. But I still love it.

Don't ask. Buy it. Run 3 first tutorials. Make a break. Insert headphones. Turn on Hanz Zimmer OST Interstellar. Run any mission (my recommendation is Scrapyard or Orbital). Your nearest 30 mins in VR are just perfect.
The game is perfect, the game is challenging, the game is very well aligned with its controls and feels just like good game should feel. I want more levels.

Excellent virtual control implementation, lovely aesthetic and overall very fun experience! The developers are engaged with their growing community and are very responsive to feedback and bug reports.

This is the game i have been looking for in vr. Its what vr was made for in my opinion. I feel like im a real space pilot.  With all flight assist off its just an amazing experience. Really challenging but rewarding.  Great tasks.  Can’t wait to see how they expand the game.  Would love to see some multiplayer and would love to have a local coop experience where maybe a co pilot is in the same ship but controlling a different part of the ship.

I found space docker to be fun and relaxing. Though the objectives are simple the mechanics add a layer of difficulty that will keep you entertained. The main gameplay consists of picking up payloads and delivering them to a designated area. The payloads vary from simple containers to timed explosive nukes.    Some are spinning and require you to stabilize it while others need to be chased orbiting black holes. Overall a fun little game with great visuals.
Very reallistic experience sometimes a bit frustrating because learning curve. I recommend if you are looking for some different space and spicy game.

reviews.review_title: Played on PC VR & just bought it again
reviews.review_description: 10/10 would recommend throwing a nuclear core at a tiny docking port in a space ship with 5 seconds left before it explodes

This is the kind of experience VR excels at. Having never piloted a space cargo tug boat, I can still say this is exactly the type of experience I've been waiting for ever since MS Space Simulator. Immersive, good controls.

reviews.review_title: Hidden gem that needs to be on the store
reviews.review_description: So glad I found this game, it is a refreshing change. Very immersive with great controls that are all configurable to your liking. Very challenging and highly addictive. The ships cockpit is really immersive truly giving a sense of piloting a ship through space in zero g. Using all the ships instrument readouts and docking camera are a must. Easy to pickup, difficult to master. if you're into space flight/sims this is the game for you. If you suffer from VR motion sickness this game probably wont be for you, but if you love the feeling of your stomach falling as you flip and spin while matching velocity with your moving target then you will love this game.
If you want a fun job in the deep space, this is the app for you
I love this game, very nice visuals (I also own the steam version) en sense of scale.

reviews.review_title: i had doughts but man this game is great
reviews.review_description: controls are a bit tricky but can easily be adjusted to your liking , relaxing music in the background i lost track of time and was playing for hours super concentrated and fr the price .i say buy it!!!

First off let me say this game was not for me. But I have given this 5 stars because I can appreciate this is a very well made game, with superb physics.

I kind of knew this game wouldn't be for me but I really wanted to try it as it looked interesting and thought I may actually really enjoy it.

Unfortunately I prefer quick 'jump in jump out' games with action and while I enjoyed flying the ship, I just lost patience during the docking, and believe me you need patience.

If this game is your type of game, then I can honestly say this really will float your boat and is worthy of your money.

The space physics really are outstanding, and the sound FX are realistic. Graphics are decent and fit the game perfectly.

There are plenty of options to help you control the ship and the tutorial videos are helpful and comedic.

Highly recommended if this is your type of game!

Gameplay, graphics, controls and sound effects are simply amazing. Can be relaxing or challenging, depending on the settings mode and missions.
Time is flying when playing, literally!
